title: aciphylla dissecta
intro: Aciphylla dissecta, also known as New Zealand parsley fern or mountain parsnip, is a flowering plant in the carrot family native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, offering benefits like improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, Aciphylla dissecta is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
